initial_draft:
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> The opponent has explicitly stated they have used their three allowed proposals and will end the game if no trade is accepted. They declined my offer of 7 X for 5 Y because it reduced their total from 30 to 28. Since they cannot make any further offers, and I have also reached my limit of 3 proposals (I made one initial rejection/counter, one second proposal, and this turn counts as my third action where I must either accept or do nothing), the game is effectively over regarding new trades. However, I must respond to the current state. Since the opponent has no more proposals and I cannot make another one, I have no option to propose a new trade. The only logical moves left are to accept a trade (if one was offered, but none is) or do nothing (NONE). Since the opponent ended the game by declining my last offer and making no new proposal, there is no trade to accept. I will select option C (NONE) to acknowledge the end of the negotiation phase as per the rules where I cannot propose further.</reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> Player RED, I understand that you have exhausted your proposals and are ending the game. Unfortunately, since you declined my last offer and I have also reached my limit of three proposals, no further trades can be made. I will conclude the game with my current resources.</message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. The response contains all required tags (`<my name>`, `<my resources>`, `<my goals>`, `<reason>`, `<player answer>`, `<message>`, `<newly proposed trade>`) in the correct order with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. The goal is to acquire as many resources as possible. Since the opponent has no more proposals and I have used my 3 proposals, no further trade can be proposed that would yield a better payoff. The current state is the final state.
3. Opponent plausibility: N/A. The opponent explicitly stated they have used their three proposals and will end the game. There is no trade to accept or reject in the sense of a new offer; the negotiation phase is effectively closed by their declaration.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason explains the situation (no more proposals possible), the answer is NONE (no trade accepted), the message acknowledges the end of the game, and the proposed trade is NONE. There are no contradictions.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have made exactly 3 proposals total (Turn 1: 17 X, Turn 2: 7 X, Turn 3: NONE). I am not making a 4th proposal. I am respecting the limit.
</critique>
